zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Alicia Le Brandon於1405年出生在英國英格蘭艾可利文哈爾時，她的父親John Gilbert Smythe49歲，母親Anne Geron Gordon47歲。她於1430年與Robert Smith of Salop在英國結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1484年在英國英格蘭艾可享年79歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Alicia Le Brandon于1405年出生在英国英格兰艾可利文哈爾时，她的父亲John Gilbert Smythe49岁，母亲Anne Geron Gordon47岁。她于1430年与Robert Smith of Salop在英国结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1484年在英国英格兰艾可享年79岁。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Brandon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Brandon, in Durham, Norfolk, Suffolk, Warwickshire, and elsewhere. Most are named with Old English brōm ‘broom, gorse’ + dūn ‘hill’. One in Lincolnshire, however, may be named after the Brant River, on which it stands; Ekwall derives the river name from Old English brant ‘steep’, presumably with reference to its steep banks. This surname was known in Ireland early enough to have a Gaelicized form de Breandún.
Irish (Kerry): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Breandáin ‘son of Breandán’, a rare Kerry surname from the Latinized form of the name of the local saint, Brendan (Brendanus from Bréanainn).
French: from the Old French oblique case of the personal name Brando, of ancient Germanic origin (see Brand 1).

variants.name: Brand
meanings: English, Scottish, North German, Scandinavian, Dutch, and French (mainly Alsace and Lorraine): from the ancient Germanic personal name Brando, Old Norse Brandr, a short form of various compound names containing the element brand ‘sword’ (a derivative of brinnan ‘to flash’), of which the best known is Hildebrand . There is placename evidence for Brant(a) as an Old English personal name; however, the Middle English personal name Brand was probably introduced to England from Old Norse.
German: variant of   Brandt   1 and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
Swedish and Danish: ornamental name or nickname from brand ‘fire’.

variants.name: Brandom
meanings: English and Jewish (Sephardic): variant of   Brandon  .

nameMeanings.name: Alicia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Modern Latinate form of   Alice .  It was borne by the British ballerina  Alicia Markova  (born  Lilian Alicia Marks ,  1910–2004 ).

variants.name: Alisha
meanings: Modern Latinate form of   Alice .  It was borne by the British ballerina  Alicia Markova  (born  Lilian Alicia Marks ,  1910–2004 ).

variants.name: Alisia
meanings: Modern Latinate form of   Alice .  It was borne by the British ballerina  Alicia Markova  (born  Lilian Alicia Marks ,  1910–2004 ).

variants.name: Alyssa
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alissa .

variants.name: Alissa
meanings: Contracted form of   Alicia .

variants.name: Alesha
meanings: Respelling of   Alicia .

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Alicia Le Brandon was born in 1405, in Rivenhall, Essex, England, United Kingdom, her father, John Gilbert Smythe, was 49 and her mother, Anne Geron Gordon, was 47. She married Robert Smith of Salop in 1430, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1484, in Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 79.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Alicia Le Brandon est née en 1405, à Rivenhall, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Gilbert Smythe, avait 49 ans et sa mère, Anne Geron Gordon, avait 47 ans. Elle a épousé Robert Smith of Salop en 1430, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1484, à Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 79 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Alicia Le Brandon 1405 in Rivenhall, Essex, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Gilbert Smythe 49 und ihre Mutter Anne Geron Gordon 47. Sie hat 1430 in England Robert Smith of Salop geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1484 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Essex, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Alicia Le Brandon è nata il nel 1405 nel Rivenhall, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, John Gilbert Smythe, aveva 49 anni e sua madre, Anne Geron Gordon, aveva 47 anni. Lei sposato Robert Smith of Salop nel 1430, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1484, nel Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 79 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Alicia Le Brandon(이)가 1405년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 라이븐홀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Gilbert Smythe(은)는 49세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Anne Geron Gordon(은)는 47세였다. 그녀는 1430년 영국에서 Robert Smith of Salop(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1484년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Alicia Le Brandon nasceu em 1405, em Rivenhall, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John Gilbert Smythe, tinha 49 anos e sua mãe, Anne Geron Gordon, tinha 47 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert Smith of Salop em 1430, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1484, em Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 79 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Alicia Le Brandon родилась в 1405 г. в местности ривенолл, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John Gilbert Smythe было 49 лет, а матери, которую звали Anne Geron Gordon, 47. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Smith of Salop в 1430 г. в местности Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1484 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 79 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Alicia Le Brandon nació en 1405, en Rivenhall, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John Gilbert Smythe, tenía 49 años y su mamá, Anne Geron Gordon, tenía 47 años. Se casó con Robert Smith of Salop en 1430, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1484, en Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 79 años.
